parsers.c(3) Library Functions Manual parsers.c(3)

NAME

parsers.c - Custom option parsers implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct setting_map_entry_t
Lookup table for setting string-to-enum mapping.

Functions


bool parse_color_setting (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_utf8_setting (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_color_mode (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_color_filter (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_render_mode (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_render_theme (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_palette_type (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_log_level (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_port_option (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
int parse_server_bind_address (const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)
Parse server bind address positional argument. int parse_client_address (const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)
Parse client address positional argument. int parse_mirror_media (const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)
Parse mirror mode positional argument (file path or URL). bool parse_palette_chars (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_verbose_flag (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_timestamp (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
Custom parser for --seek flag. bool parse_volume (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_log_file (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_audio_source (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)

Detailed Description

Custom option parsers implementation.

Definition in file parsers.c.

Function Documentation

bool parse_audio_source (const char * arg, void * dest, char ** error_msg)

Definition at line 1150 of file parsers.c.

1150                                                                        {
1151   if (!arg || !dest) {
1152     if (error_msg) {
1153       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
1154     }
1155     return false;
1156   }
1157 
1158   audio_source_t *audio_source = (audio_source_t *)dest;
1159   char lower[32];
1160   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
1161 
1162   // Auto (smart selection based on media state)
1163   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_AUDIO_SOURCE_AUTO) == 0) {
1164     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_AUTO;
1165     return true;
1166   }
1167 
1168   // Microphone only
1169   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_AUDIO_SOURCE_MIC) == 0) {
1170     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_MIC;
1171     return true;
1172   }
1173 
1174   // Media only
1175   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_AUDIO_SOURCE_MEDIA) == 0) {
1176     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_MEDIA;
1177     return true;
1178   }
1179 
1180   // Both microphone and media
1181   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_AUDIO_SOURCE_BOTH) == 0) {
1182     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_BOTH;
1183     return true;
1184   }
1185 
1186   if (error_msg) {
1187     *error_msg = platform_strdup("Audio source must be 'auto', 'mic', 'media', or 'both'");
1188   }
1189   return false;
1190 }




References platform_strdup().






int
  parse_client_address (const char * arg, void * config, char ** remaining, int
  num_remaining, char ** error_msg)


Parse client address positional argument. Implements the client
    address parsing logic from client.c. Parses [address][:port] with complex
    IPv6 handling.


Definition at line 674 of file parsers.c.


674                                                                                                                {
675   (void)remaining;
676   (void)num_remaining;
677 
678   if (!arg || !config) {
679     if (error_msg) {
680       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or config");
681     }
682     return -1;
683   }
684 
685   log_debug("parse_client_address: Processing argument: '%s'", arg);
686 
687   // Access address and port fields from options_state struct
688   char *address = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, address);
689   int *port = (int *)((char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, port));
690 
691   // Check for WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://) FIRST before session string validation
692   // WebSocket URLs are passed through without validation or port extraction
693   if (strncmp(arg, "ws://", 5) == 0 || strncmp(arg, "wss://", 6) == 0) {
694     log_debug("Detected WebSocket URL: %s", arg);
695     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
696     // For wss://, TLS handles transport encryption - allow ACIP to handle auth independently
697     // For ws://, default to ACIP encryption (no TLS)
698     if (strncmp(arg, "wss://", 6) == 0) {
699       bool *no_encrypt = (bool *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, no_encrypt);
700       *no_encrypt = true;
701       log_debug("Auto-detected wss:// - setting no_encrypt=true (TLS handles transport encryption, ACIP handles auth)");
702     }
703     // Don't set port - WebSocket transport handles URL parsing internally
704     return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
705   }
706 
707   // Check for TCP URL (tcp://) - passed through directly to FFmpeg
708   // TCP URLs are used for streaming media from custom TCP servers
709   if (strncmp(arg, "tcp://", 6) == 0) {
710     log_debug("Detected TCP URL: %s", arg);
711     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
712     // Don't set port - TCP transport handles URL parsing internally
713     return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
714   }
715 
716   // Check if this is a session string (format: adjective-noun-noun)
717   // Session strings have exactly 2 hyphens, only lowercase letters, length 5-47
718   bool is_session = is_session_string(arg);
719   log_debug("parse_client_address: is_session_string('%s') = %s", arg, is_session ? "true" : "false");
720 
721   if (is_session) {
722     // This is a session string, not a server address
723     char *session_string = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, session_string);
724     SAFE_SNPRINTF(session_string, SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s", arg);
725     log_debug("parse_client_address: Stored session string: %s", arg);
726     return 1; // Consumed 1 arg
727   }
728 
729   // Not a session string, parse as server address
730   log_debug("parse_client_address: Parsing as server address (not a session string)");
731 
732   // Check for port in address (format: address:port or [ipv6]:port)
733   const char *colon = strrchr(arg, ':');
734 
735   if (colon != NULL) {
736     // Check if this is IPv6 with port [::1]:port or plain hostname:port
737     if (arg[0] == '[') {
738       // IPv6 with brackets: [address]:port
739       const char *closing_bracket = strchr(arg, ']');
740       if (closing_bracket && closing_bracket < colon) {
741         // Extract address (remove brackets)
742         size_t addr_len = (size_t)(closing_bracket - arg - 1);
743         if (addr_len >= OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE) {
744           if (error_msg) {
745             *error_msg = platform_strdup("IPv6 address too long");
746           }
747           return -1;
748         }
749         SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%.*s", (int)addr_len, arg + 1);
750 
751         // Extract and validate port
752         const char *port_str = colon + 1;
753         char *endptr;
754         long port_num = strtol(port_str, &endptr, 10);
755         if (*endptr != '\0' || port_num < 1 || port_num > 65535) {
756           if (error_msg) {
757             char msg[256];
758             safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid port number '%s'. Must be 1-65535.", port_str);
759             *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
760           }
761           return -1;
762         }
763         *port = (int)port_num;
764       }
765     } else {
766       // Check if it's IPv6 without brackets (no port allowed)
767       // or hostname/IPv4:port
768       size_t colon_count = 0;
769       for (const char *p = arg; *p; p++) {
770         if (*p == ':')
771           colon_count++;
772       }
773 
774       if (colon_count == 1) {
775         // Likely hostname:port or IPv4:port
776         size_t addr_len = (size_t)(colon - arg);
777         if (addr_len >= OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE) {
778           if (error_msg) {
779             *error_msg = platform_strdup("Address too long");
780           }
781           return -1;
782         }
783         SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%.*s", (int)addr_len, arg);
784 
785         // Extract and validate port
786         const char *port_str = colon + 1;
787         char *endptr;
788         long port_num = strtol(port_str, &endptr, 10);
789         if (*endptr != '\0' || port_num < 1 || port_num > 65535) {
790           if (error_msg) {
791             char msg[256];
792             safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid port number '%s'. Must be 1-65535.", port_str);
793             *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
794           }
795           return -1;
796         }
797         *port = (int)port_num;
798       } else {
799         // Multiple colons - likely bare IPv6 address
800         SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
801       }
802     }
803   } else {
804     // No colon - just an address
805     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
806   }
807 
808   // Validate addresses using comprehensive IPv4/IPv6 detection
809   bool has_dot = strchr(address, '.') != NULL;
810   bool has_colon = strchr(address, ':') != NULL;
811   bool starts_with_digit = address[0] >= '0' && address[0] <= '9';
812 
813   // Potential IPv6 address (has colons) - validate as IPv6
814   if (has_colon) {
815     if (!is_valid_ipv6(address)) {
816       if (error_msg) {
817         char msg[512];
818         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
819                       "Invalid IPv6 address '%s'.\n"
820                       "IPv6 addresses must be valid hex notation with colons.\n"
821                       "Examples: ::1, 2001:db8::1, fe80::1\n"
822                       "Or use hostnames like example.com",
823                       address);
824         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
825       }
826       return -1;
827     }
828   } else if (has_dot && starts_with_digit) {
829     // Potential IPv4 address (has dots and starts with digit) - validate strictly
830     if (!is_valid_ipv4(address)) {
831       if (error_msg) {
832         char msg[512];
833         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
834                       "Invalid IPv4 address '%s'.\n"
835                       "IPv4 addresses must have exactly 4 octets (0-255) separated by dots.\n"
836                       "Examples: 127.0.0.1, 192.168.1.1\n"
837                       "For hostnames, use letters: example.com, localhost",
838                       address);
839         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
840       }
841       return -1;
842     }
843   }
844   // Otherwise treat as valid hostname (no validation needed)
845 
846   // Note: Port conflict checking would require additional state
847   // (checking if --port flag was used). For now, this is a simplified version.
848   // Full implementation would need to track whether port was set via flag.
849 
850   log_debug("parse_client_address: Set address='%s', port=%d", address[0] ? address : "(empty)", *port);
851 
852   return 1; // Consumed 1 arg
853 }






References is_session_string(), is_valid_ipv4(),
    is_valid_ipv6(), platform_strdup(), safe_snprintf(),
    and SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE.

bool parse_color_filter (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 270 of file parsers.c.


270                                                                        {
271   if (!arg || !dest) {
272     if (error_msg) {
273       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
274     }
275     return false;
276   }
277 
278   color_filter_t *color_filter = (color_filter_t *)dest;
279   char lower[32];
280   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
281 
282   // Try to match against all known color filters
283   *color_filter = color_filter_from_cli_name(lower);
284   if (*color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE || strcmp(lower, OPT_COLOR_FILTER_NONE) == 0) {
285     return true;
286   }
287 
288   // Invalid value
289   if (error_msg) {
290     char msg[256];
291     safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
292                   "Invalid color filter '%s'. Valid values: none, black, white, green, magenta, fuchsia, "
293                   "orange, teal, cyan, pink, red, yellow",
294                   arg);
295     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
296   }
297   return false;
298 }

bool parse_color_mode (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Definition at line 211 of file parsers.c.


211                                                                      {
212   if (!arg || !dest) {
213     if (error_msg) {
214       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
215     }
216     return false;
217   }
218 
219   terminal_color_mode_t *color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t *)dest;
220   char lower[32];
221   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
222 
223   // Auto-detect
224   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_COLOR_MODE_AUTO) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "a") == 0) {
225     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
226     return true;
227   }
228 
229   // Monochrome/None
230   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_COLOR_MODE_NONE) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "mono") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "monochrome") == 0 ||
231       strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
232     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
233     return true;
234   }
235 
236   // 16-color
237   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_COLOR_MODE_16) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "16color") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "ansi") == 0 ||
238       strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
239     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_16;
240     return true;
241   }
242 
243   // 256-color
244   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_COLOR_MODE_256) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "256color") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
245     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_256;
246     return true;
247   }
248 
249   // Truecolor
250   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_COLOR_MODE_TRUECOLOR) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "true") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "tc") == 0 ||
251       strcmp(lower, "rgb") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "24bit") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "3") == 0) {
252     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR;
253     return true;
254   }
255 
256   // Invalid value - suggest closest match
257   if (error_msg) {
258     char msg[256];
259     const char *suggestion = asciichat_suggest_enum_value("color-mode", arg);
260     if (suggestion) {
261       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid color mode '%s'. Did you mean '%s'?", arg, suggestion);
262     } else {
263       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid color mode '%s'. Valid values: auto, none, 16, 256, truecolor", arg);
264     }
265     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
266   }
267   return false;
268 }

    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().






bool parse_color_setting (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 161 of file parsers.c.


161                                                                         {
162   if (!dest) {
163     if (error_msg) {
164       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL destination");
165     }
166     return false;
167   }
168 
169   // Use generic parser with color setting lookup table
170   // Default to TRUE if no arg provided
171   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
172     int *color_setting = (int *)dest;
173     *color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
174     return true;
175   }
176 
177   return parse_setting_generic(arg, dest, g_color_setting_map, error_msg);
178 }

bool parse_log_file (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Definition at line 1116 of file parsers.c.


1116                                                                    {
1117   if (!arg || !dest) {
1118     if (error_msg) {
1119       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
1120     }
1121     return false;
1122   }
1123 
1124   // Validate and normalize the log file path
1125   char *normalized = NULL;
1126   asciichat_error_t result = path_validate_user_path(arg, PATH_ROLE_LOG_FILE, &normalized);
1127 
1128   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1129     if (error_msg) {
1130       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1131       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1132         *error_msg = platform_strdup(err_ctx.context_message);
1133       } else {
1134         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Log file path validation failed");
1135       }
1136     }
1137     return false;
1138   }
1139 
1140   // Copy validated path to destination
1141   char *log_file_buf = (char *)dest;
1142   const size_t max_size = 256;
1143   SAFE_STRNCPY(log_file_buf, normalized, max_size - 1);
1144   log_file_buf[max_size - 1] = '\0';
1145 
1146   SAFE_FREE(normalized);
1147   return true;
1148 }

bool parse_log_level (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Definition at line 447 of file parsers.c.


447                                                                     {
448   if (!dest) {
449     if (error_msg) {
450       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL destination");
451     }
452     return false;
453   }
454 
455   log_level_t *log_level = (log_level_t *)dest;
456 
457   // If no argument provided, use the default log level (based on build type)
458   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
459     *log_level = DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL;
460     return true;
461   }
462 
463   char lower[32];
464   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
465 
466   // Development level
467   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_LOG_LEVEL_DEV) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "development") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
468     *log_level = LOG_DEV;
469     return true;
470   }
471 
472   // Debug level
473   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "dbg") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
474     *log_level = LOG_DEBUG;
475     return true;
476   }
477 
478   // Info level
479   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_LOG_LEVEL_INFO) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "information") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
480     *log_level = LOG_INFO;
481     return true;
482   }
483 
484   // Warning level
485   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_LOG_LEVEL_WARN) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "warning") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "3") == 0) {
486     *log_level = LOG_WARN;
487     return true;
488   }
489 
490   // Error level
491   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_LOG_LEVEL_ERROR) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "err") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "4") == 0) {
492     *log_level = LOG_ERROR;
493     return true;
494   }
495 
496   // Fatal level
497   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_LOG_LEVEL_FATAL) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "5") == 0) {
498     *log_level = LOG_FATAL;
499     return true;
500   }
501 
502   // Invalid value
503   if (error_msg) {
504     char msg[256];
505     safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid log level '%s'. Valid values: dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal", arg);
506     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
507   }
508   return false;
509 }






References platform_strdup(), and
  safe_snprintf().


Referenced by options_init().






int
  parse_mirror_media (const char * arg, void * config, char ** remaining, int
  num_remaining, char ** error_msg)


Parse mirror mode positional argument (file path or URL).
  Logic:



  
1.

  
Enforce mutual exclusion with --file and --url flags

  
2.

  
Check if argument is an accessible file path first

  
3.

  
If not a file, treat as URL (yt-dlp/ffmpeg will handle errors)

  
4.

  
Set media_file or media_url accordingly




Definition at line 868 of file parsers.c.


868                                                                                                              {
869   (void)remaining;
870   (void)num_remaining;
871 
872   if (!arg || !config) {
873     if (error_msg) {
874       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or config");
875     }
876     return -1;
877   }
878 
879   log_debug("parse_mirror_media: Processing argument: '%s'", arg);
880 
881   // Access media_file and media_url fields from options_state struct (same as other positional parsers)
882   char *media_file = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, media_file);
883   char *media_url = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, media_url);
884 
885   // Enforce mutual exclusion with --file flag
886   if (media_file[0] != '\0') {
887     if (error_msg) {
888       *error_msg = platform_strdup("cannot use both --file and a positional argument");
889     }
890     return -1;
891   }
892 
893   // Enforce mutual exclusion with --url flag
894   if (media_url[0] != '\0') {
895     if (error_msg) {
896       *error_msg = platform_strdup("cannot use both --url and a positional argument");
897     }
898     return -1;
899   }
900 
901   // Check if it's an accessible file path (priority 1)
902   if (platform_access(arg, PLATFORM_ACCESS_READ) == 0) {
903     log_debug("Argument '%s' is an accessible file", arg);
904     SAFE_SNPRINTF(media_file, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
905     return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
906   }
907 
908   // Not a file; treat as URL (yt-dlp/ffmpeg will handle any errors)
909   log_debug("Argument '%s' not found as file; treating as URL", arg);
910   SAFE_SNPRINTF(media_url, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
911   return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
912 }






References platform_access(), and
  platform_strdup().


Referenced by options_preset_unified().






bool parse_palette_chars (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 918 of file parsers.c.


918                                                                         {
919   if (!arg || !dest) {
920     if (error_msg) {
921       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
922     }
923     return false;
924   }
925 
926   // The dest pointer points to the palette_custom field in options_t
927   // We need to get the full options_t struct to call parse_palette_chars_option
928   // Since we only have the field pointer, we need to handle this directly
929 
930   char *palette_custom = (char *)dest;
931 
932   size_t len = strlen(arg);
933   if (len == 0) {
934     if (error_msg) {
935       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid palette-chars: value cannot be empty");
936     }
937     return false;
938   }
939 
940   if (len >= 256) {
941     if (error_msg) {
942       char msg[256];
943       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid palette-chars: too long (%zu chars, max 255)", len);
944       *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
945     }
946     return false;
947   }
948 
949   // Copy the palette characters
950   SAFE_STRNCPY(palette_custom, arg, 256);
951   palette_custom[255] = '\0';
952 
953   // Also set the palette type to custom by calculating back to options_t pointer
954   // dest points to options_t.palette_custom, so we can get options_t* using offset arithmetic
955   options_t *opts = (options_t *)((char *)dest - offsetof(options_t, palette_custom));
956   opts->palette_type = PALETTE_CUSTOM;
957 
958   return true;
959 }






References platform_strdup(), and
  safe_snprintf().






bool parse_palette_type (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 384 of file parsers.c.


384                                                                        {
385   if (!arg || !dest) {
386     if (error_msg) {
387       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
388     }
389     return false;
390   }
391 
392   palette_type_t *palette_type = (palette_type_t *)dest;
393   char lower[32];
394   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
395 
396   // Standard palette
397   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_PALETTE_STANDARD) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "std") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
398     *palette_type = PALETTE_STANDARD;
399     return true;
400   }
401 
402   // Blocks palette
403   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_PALETTE_BLOCKS) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "block") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
404     *palette_type = PALETTE_BLOCKS;
405     return true;
406   }
407 
408   // Digital palette
409   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_PALETTE_DIGITAL) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "dig") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
410     *palette_type = PALETTE_DIGITAL;
411     return true;
412   }
413 
414   // Minimal palette
415   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_PALETTE_MINIMAL) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "min") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "3") == 0) {
416     *palette_type = PALETTE_MINIMAL;
417     return true;
418   }
419 
420   // Cool palette
421   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_PALETTE_COOL) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "4") == 0) {
422     *palette_type = PALETTE_COOL;
423     return true;
424   }
425 
426   // Custom palette
427   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_PALETTE_CUSTOM) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "5") == 0) {
428     *palette_type = PALETTE_CUSTOM;
429     return true;
430   }
431 
432   // Invalid value - suggest closest match
433   if (error_msg) {
434     char msg[256];
435     const char *suggestion = asciichat_suggest_enum_value("palette", arg);
436     if (suggestion) {
437       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid palette type '%s'. Did you mean '%s'?", arg, suggestion);
438     } else {
439       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
440                     "Invalid palette type '%s'. Valid values: standard, blocks, digital, minimal, cool, custom", arg);
441     }
442     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
443   }
444   return false;
445 }






References asciichat_suggest_enum_value(),
    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().


Referenced by set_palette().






bool parse_port_option (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 511 of file parsers.c.


511                                                                       {
512   if (!arg || !dest) {
513     if (error_msg) {
514       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
515     }
516     return false;
517   }
518 
519   int *port_value = (int *)dest;
520   uint16_t port_num;
521 
522   // Use the existing parse_port function for validation
523   asciichat_error_t err = parse_port(arg, &port_num);
524   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
525     if (error_msg) {
526       char msg[256];
527       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid port '%s'. Port must be a number between 0 and 65535 (0 = auto-assign).",
528                     arg);
529       *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
530     }
531     return false;
532   }
533 
534   *port_value = (int)port_num;
535   return true;
536 }






References parse_port(), platform_strdup(), and
    safe_snprintf().






bool parse_render_mode (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 300 of file parsers.c.


300                                                                       {
301   if (!arg || !dest) {
302     if (error_msg) {
303       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
304     }
305     return false;
306   }
307 
308   render_mode_t *render_mode = (render_mode_t *)dest;
309   char lower[32];
310   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
311 
312   // Foreground mode
313   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND) == 0 || strcmp(lower, OPT_RENDER_MODE_FG) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
314     *render_mode = RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND;
315     return true;
316   }
317 
318   // Background mode
319   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND) == 0 || strcmp(lower, OPT_RENDER_MODE_BG) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
320     *render_mode = RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND;
321     return true;
322   }
323 
324   // Half-block mode
325   if (strcmp(lower, OPT_RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK) == 0 || strcmp(lower, "hb") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
326     *render_mode = RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK;
327     return true;
328   }
329 
330   // Invalid value - suggest closest match
331   if (error_msg) {
332     char msg[256];
333     const char *suggestion = asciichat_suggest_enum_value("render-mode", arg);
334     if (suggestion) {
335       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid render mode '%s'. Did you mean '%s'?", arg, suggestion);
336     } else {
337       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid render mode '%s'. Valid values: foreground, background, half-block",
338                     arg);
339     }
340     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
341   }
342   return false;
343 }






References asciichat_suggest_enum_value(),
    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().






bool parse_render_theme (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 345 of file parsers.c.


345                                                                        {
346   if (!arg || !dest) {
347     if (error_msg) {
348       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
349     }
350     return false;
351   }
352 
353   int *theme = (int *)dest;
354   char lower[32];
355   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
356 
357   // Dark theme
358   if (strcmp(lower, "dark") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
359     *theme = 0; // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK
360     return true;
361   }
362 
363   // Light theme
364   if (strcmp(lower, "light") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
365     *theme = 1; // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT
366     return true;
367   }
368 
369   // Auto theme
370   if (strcmp(lower, "auto") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
371     *theme = 2; // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO
372     return true;
373   }
374 
375   // Invalid value
376   if (error_msg) {
377     char msg[256];
378     safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid render theme '%s'. Valid values: dark, light, auto", arg);
379     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
380   }
381   return false;
382 }






References platform_strdup(), and
  safe_snprintf().






int
  parse_server_bind_address (const char * arg, void * config, char ** remaining,
  int num_remaining, char ** error_msg)


Parse server bind address positional argument. Implements the
    server bind address parsing logic from server.c. Can consume 1 argument per
    call, handling IPv4 or IPv6 bind addresses. The positional arg system will
    call this multiple times for multiple args.


Definition at line 552 of file parsers.c.


552                                                                                                                     {
553   if (!arg || !config) {
554     if (error_msg) {
555       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or config");
556     }
557     return -1;
558   }
559 
560   // Assume config struct has address and address6 fields (OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE each)
561   // This is a simplified version that assumes standard options_state layout
562   char *address = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, address);
563   char *address6 = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, address6);
564 
565   int consumed = 0;
566 
567   // Parse first argument (IPv4 or IPv6)
568   char parsed_addr[OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE];
569   const char *addr_to_check = arg;
570   if (parse_ipv6_address(arg, parsed_addr, sizeof(parsed_addr)) == 0) {
571     addr_to_check = parsed_addr;
572   }
573 
574   // Check if it's IPv4 or IPv6
575   if (is_valid_ipv4(addr_to_check)) {
576     // Check if we already have a non-default IPv4 address
577     // Allow overwriting defaults (localhost, 0.0.0.0)
578     if (address[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv4(address) && strcmp(address, "localhost") != 0 &&
579         strcmp(address, "0.0.0.0") != 0) {
580       if (error_msg) {
581         char msg[256];
582         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
583                       "Cannot specify multiple IPv4 addresses.\n"
584                       "Already have: %s\n"
585                       "Cannot add: %s",
586                       address, addr_to_check);
587         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
588       }
589       return -1;
590     }
591     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
592     consumed = 1;
593   } else if (is_valid_ipv6(addr_to_check)) {
594     // Check if we already have a non-default IPv6 address
595     // Allow overwriting default (::1)
596     if (address6[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv6(address6)) {
597       if (error_msg) {
598         char msg[256];
599         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
600                       "Cannot specify multiple IPv6 addresses.\n"
601                       "Already have: %s\n"
602                       "Cannot add: %s",
603                       address6, addr_to_check);
604         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
605       }
606       return -1;
607     }
608     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address6, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
609     consumed = 1;
610   } else {
611     if (error_msg) {
612       char msg[512];
613       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
614                     "Invalid IP address '%s'.\n"
615                     "Server bind addresses must be valid IPv4 or IPv6 addresses.\n"
616                     "Examples:\n"
617                     "  ascii-chat server 0.0.0.0\n"
618                     "  ascii-chat server ::1\n"
619                     "  ascii-chat server 0.0.0.0 ::1",
620                     arg);
621       *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
622     }
623     return -1;
624   }
625 
626   // Try to parse second address if available
627   if (remaining && num_remaining > 0 && remaining[0]) {
628     const char *second_arg = remaining[0];
629     memset(parsed_addr, 0, sizeof(parsed_addr));
630     addr_to_check = second_arg;
631     if (parse_ipv6_address(second_arg, parsed_addr, sizeof(parsed_addr)) == 0) {
632       addr_to_check = parsed_addr;
633     }
634 
635     if (is_valid_ipv4(addr_to_check)) {
636       // Second is IPv4
637       if (address[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv4(address) && strcmp(address, "localhost") != 0 &&
638           strcmp(address, "0.0.0.0") != 0) {
639         // Already have an IPv4, can't add another
640         return consumed;
641       }
642       if (is_valid_ipv4(arg)) {
643         // First was also IPv4, can't have two IPv4s
644         return consumed;
645       }
646       // First was IPv6, second is IPv4 - accept both
647       SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
648       consumed = 2;
649     } else if (is_valid_ipv6(addr_to_check)) {
650       // Second is IPv6
651       if (address6[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv6(address6)) {
652         // Already have an IPv6, can't add another
653         return consumed;
654       }
655       if (is_valid_ipv6(arg)) {
656         // First was also IPv6, can't have two IPv6s
657         return consumed;
658       }
659       // First was IPv4, second is IPv6 - accept both
660       SAFE_SNPRINTF(address6, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
661       consumed = 2;
662     }
663   }
664 
665   return consumed;
666 }






References is_localhost_ipv4(), is_localhost_ipv6(),
    is_valid_ipv4(), is_valid_ipv6(), parse_ipv6_address(),
    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().


Referenced by options_preset_unified().






bool parse_timestamp (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Custom parser for --seek flag. Accepts both
    "hh:mm:ss.ms" format and plain seconds format. Examples:


    
  
  • "30" = 30 seconds
    • 
  
  • "30.5" = 30.5 seconds
    • 
  
  • "1:30" = 1 minute 30 seconds (90 seconds)
    • 
  
  • "1:30.5" = 1 minute 30.5 seconds (90.5 seconds)
    • 
  
  • "0:1:30.5" = 1 minute 30.5 seconds (90.5 seconds)
    • 
  
  • "1:2:30.5" = 1 hour 2 minutes 30.5 seconds (3750.5 seconds)
    • 



Definition at line 999 of file parsers.c.


999                                                                     {
1000   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
1001     if (error_msg) {
1002       *error_msg = platform_strdup("--seek requires a timestamp argument");
1003     }
1004     return false;
1005   }
1006 
1007   double *timestamp = (double *)dest;
1008   char *endptr;
1009   long strtol_result;
1010 
1011   // Count colons to determine format
1012   int colon_count = 0;
1013   for (const char *p = arg; *p; p++) {
1014     if (*p == ':')
1015       colon_count++;
1016   }
1017 
1018   if (colon_count == 0) {
1019     // Plain seconds format: "30" or "30.5"
1020     *timestamp = strtod(arg, &endptr);
1021     if (*endptr != '\0' || *timestamp < 0.0) {
1022       if (error_msg) {
1023         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected non-negative seconds");
1024       }
1025       return false;
1026     }
1027     return true;
1028   } else if (colon_count == 1) {
1029     // MM:SS or MM:SS.ms format
1030     strtol_result = strtol(arg, &endptr, 10);
1031     if (*endptr != ':' || strtol_result < 0) {
1032       if (error_msg) {
1033         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected MM:SS or MM:SS.ms format");
1034       }
1035       return false;
1036     }
1037     long minutes = strtol_result;
1038     double seconds = strtod(endptr + 1, &endptr);
1039     if (*endptr != '\0' && *endptr != '.' && *endptr != '\0') {
1040       if (error_msg) {
1041         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected MM:SS or MM:SS.ms format");
1042       }
1043       return false;
1044     }
1045     *timestamp = minutes * 60.0 + seconds;
1046     return true;
1047   } else if (colon_count == 2) {
1048     // HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.ms format
1049     strtol_result = strtol(arg, &endptr, 10);
1050     if (*endptr != ':' || strtol_result < 0) {
1051       if (error_msg) {
1052         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.ms format");
1053       }
1054       return false;
1055     }
1056     long hours = strtol_result;
1057 
1058     strtol_result = strtol(endptr + 1, &endptr, 10);
1059     if (*endptr != ':' || strtol_result < 0 || strtol_result >= 60) {
1060       if (error_msg) {
1061         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: minutes must be 0-59");
1062       }
1063       return false;
1064     }
1065     long minutes = strtol_result;
1066 
1067     double seconds = strtod(endptr + 1, &endptr);
1068     if (*endptr != '\0') {
1069       if (error_msg) {
1070         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.ms format");
1071       }
1072       return false;
1073     }
1074     *timestamp = hours * (double)SEC_PER_HOUR + minutes * (double)SEC_PER_MIN + seconds;
1075     return true;
1076   } else {
1077     if (error_msg) {
1078       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp format: too many colons");
1079     }
1080     return false;
1081   }
1082 }






References platform_strdup().






bool parse_utf8_setting (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 192 of file parsers.c.


192                                                                        {
193   if (!dest) {
194     if (error_msg) {
195       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL destination");
196     }
197     return false;
198   }
199 
200   // Use generic parser with UTF-8 setting lookup table
201   // Default to TRUE if no arg provided
202   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
203     int *utf8_setting = (int *)dest;
204     *utf8_setting = UTF8_SETTING_TRUE;
205     return true;
206   }
207 
208   return parse_setting_generic(arg, dest, g_utf8_setting_map, error_msg);
209 }






References platform_strdup().






bool parse_verbose_flag (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 961 of file parsers.c.


961                                                                        {
962   (void)error_msg; // Unused but required by function signature
963 
964   // If arg is NULL or starts with a flag, just increment
965   // Otherwise try to parse as integer count
966   unsigned short int *verbose_level = (unsigned short int *)dest;
967 
968   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
969     // No argument provided, just increment
970     (*verbose_level)++;
971     return true;
972   }
973 
974   // Try to parse as integer count
975   char *endptr;
976   long value = strtol(arg, &endptr, 10);
977   if (*endptr == '\0' && value >= 0 && value <= 100) {
978     *verbose_level = (unsigned short int)value;
979     return true;
980   }
981 
982   // If it didn't parse as int, treat as flag increment
983   (*verbose_level)++;
984   return true;
985 }










bool parse_volume (const char * arg, void * dest, char **
  error_msg)


Definition at line 1084 of file parsers.c.


1084                                                                  {
1085   if (!arg || !dest) {
1086     if (error_msg) {
1087       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
1088     }
1089     return false;
1090   }
1091 
1092   float *volume = (float *)dest;
1093   char *endptr;
1094   float val = strtof(arg, &endptr);
1095 
1096   if (*endptr != '\0' || arg == endptr) {
1097     if (error_msg) {
1098       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid volume value. Must be a number between 0.0 and 1.0");
1099     }
1100     return false;
1101   }
1102 
1103   if (val < 0.0f || val > 1.0f) {
1104     if (error_msg) {
1105       char buf[256];
1106       SAFE_SNPRINTF(buf, sizeof(buf), "Volume must be between 0.0 and 1.0 (got %.2f)", val);
1107       *error_msg = platform_strdup(buf);
1108     }
1109     return false;
1110   }
1111 
1112   *volume = val;
1113   return true;
1114 }






References platform_strdup().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
